name: hcportal_getCountries
description: Returns a list of all countries from the HC.Country table, ordered alphabetically by CountryName. Simple read-only lookup SP used for populating country dropdowns in the portal. Requires standard authentication (publicHasherId + accessToken).

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: the authenticated user's public hasher ID. Validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN function.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN.

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Used in LOG.GeneralLog message field.

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Stored in LOG.GeneralLog data field. Falls back to a placeholder JSON if NULL.

rowsets.description: Uses errorType codes 2 (validation) and 3 (auth). Validation does not short-circuit — multiple error rowsets may be returned if both publicHasherId and accessToken validations fail.

rowsets.name: CountriesResult
rowsets.description: List of all countries ordered alphabetically by CountryName. Only returned if authentication succeeds and no validation errors occurred.

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.ErrorLog on validation/auth failures
INSERT into LOG.GeneralLog on every call (after validation, with IP data and error title if validation failed)

codeSmells.detail: SET NOCOUNT ON is present (line 17) but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. Runtime errors will not auto-rollback.
The entire SP has no error handling. If any runtime error occurs (e.g., Country table unavailable), the error propagates unhandled to the caller.

Lines 23-54: All validation blocks run regardless of prior failures. Each failed check SELECTs an error rowset but does not immediately RETURN. If both publicHasherId and accessToken validations fail, the caller receives two error rowsets before the single RETURN at line 71. The caller likely only reads the first rowset.

codeSmells.issue: DATALENGTH instead of LEN for string checks
codeSmells.detail: Line 23: Uses DATALENGTH(@publicHasherId) != 16 to validate UNIQUEIDENTIFIER. While correct for GUID validation (GUIDs are 16 bytes), this is inconsistent with the standard practice of using LEN() for string length checks. For UNIQUEIDENTIFIER, a NULL check alone would suffice since non-NULL GUIDs are always 16 bytes.

Lines 29, 44, 56: Multiple INSERT operations (into HC.ErrorLog and LOG.GeneralLog) are executed without a surrounding transaction. While these are logging operations and failure is non-critical, it's inconsistent with HC6 standards.

@ipAddress and @ipGeoDetails are accepted parameters but only used for logging (lines 64-66). They have no functional impact on the SP's core behavior. This is acceptable for read-only audit logging but should be noted.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
